summary: Large fighter collision decomp PR that turned most of `src/melee/ft/ft_081B.c` from placeholders/UNK prototypes into typed C implementations. The decomp.dev bot reported +7696 matched bytes, 33 new function matches, and 3 improved near-matches, with one small regression: `ft_800850B4` dropped from 100% to 98.64%. The PR also typed related collision/map prototypes, added `mpColl_800476B4`, corrected `CollData.x60` to `float`, introduced missing MPCOLL flag constants, and updated Dash/Run collision calls to pass the fighter gobj into `ft_800844EC`. Author noted that many functions share similar structures, so severe inlining may still be involved.

key_files.role: main matching work; added many previously unmatched fighter collision/ground/ledge/wall transition functions and inline helper patterns
replaced several `UNK_RET`, `UNK_PARAMS`, and `void*` declarations with typed `Fighter_GObj*`, `ftCollisionBox*`, bool callbacks, and `HSD_GObjEvent` signatures
implemented matching `mpColl_800476B4` and minor formatting/type include support
typed many mp collision functions as returning `bool` and accepting `CollData*`, callback, and `Fighter_GObj*` arguments
replaced several `UNK_T` map library prototypes with `int`/`int*` signatures
added/documented `MPCOLL_FLAGS_B28`, `MPCOLL_FLAGS_B29`, and comments for B23-B25 bit masks
changed `CollData.x60` from `s32` to `float`, consistent with assigning `fp->input.lstick.x`

what_changed: Implemented many `ft_081B.c` functions that were previously marker comments or unknown declarations, including `ft_80082F28`, `ft_80083090`, `ft_800831CC`, `ft_80083318`, `ft_80083464`, `ft_800835B0`, `ft_8008370C`, `ft_80083844`, `ft_80083910`, `ft_80083A48`, `ft_80083C00`, `ft_80083CE4`, `ft_80083DCC`, `ft_80083E64`, `ft_8008403C`, `ft_80084104`, `ft_800841B8`, `ft_80084280`, and later functions shown in the bot report such as `ft_800844EC`, `ft_800846B0`, `ft_800848DC`, `ft_80084A80`, and `ft_80084E1C`.

Introduced repeated static inline helper bodies for closely related collision checks, including helpers around `mpColl_80047AC8/80047E14`, `mpColl_80047BF4/80047F40`, `mpColl_80047D20/8004806C`, `mpColl_800473CC`, `mpColl_800474E0`, `mpColl_80048654`, and `mpColl_8004B4B0` patterns.

Normalized repeated fighter collision state update sequence: set `coll->prev_topn`, set `coll->cur_topn` from `fp->cur_pos`, run an `mpColl_*` routine, copy `coll->cur_topn` back to `fp->cur_pos`, then gate callbacks/transitions through `ft_80081A00` and the collision result.

Added handling around ground/ledge/wall/cliff/fall transitions using existing functions such as `ft_CheckGroundAndLedge`, `ftWallJump_8008169C`, `ftCliffCommon_80081298`, `ftCo_Landing_Enter_Basic`, `ftCo_Fall_Enter`, `ftCo_8009A3C8`, and `ftCo_8009EF68`.
Replaced some raw environment flag masks with named constants, notably `MPCOLL_UNK`, `MPCOLL_FLAGS_B24`, and `MPCOLL_FLAGS_B25`; added `MPCOLL_FLAGS_B28` and `MPCOLL_FLAGS_B29` in `common_structs.h`.

Implemented `mpColl_800476B4`, including ECB setup/restore behavior, `mpColl_804D649C` small-ECB check, global callback storage through `mpColl_804D64A0/804D64A4`, `mpColl_80043754`, and `mpColl_80043324`.
Updated `ftCo_Dash_Coll` and `ftCo_Run_Coll` because `ft_800844EC` now has a typed `Fighter_GObj* gobj` parameter.
Bot evidence: 33 new matches, 3 improved unmatched functions, +7696 matched bytes, +0.20% matched code for GALE01.
smart_moves: Factored repeated collision-check structures into `static inline bool` helpers, matching the author's note that the file has many similar structures and may involve severe inlining.

Used typed callback signatures (`bool (*)(Fighter_GObj*, enum_t)` and `HSD_GObjEvent`) instead of leaving functions as generic `UNK_PARAMS`, making call sites in fighter and mp collision code clearer and reusable.
Preserved matching-sensitive stack layout with explicit `PAD_STACK(8)` and `PAD_STACK(16)` where needed.
Reused already-matched helper logic where possible, such as simplifying `ft_80082C74` to call `ft_80081D0C(gobj)` before invoking its callback.
Converted raw numeric collision masks to named constants where evidence existed, improving readability while keeping bit-exact behavior.
Changed `CollData.x60` to `float` to fit observed uses with stick input, avoiding casts or misleading integer typing.
Kept collision result checks explicit in many functions (`if (temp) return true; return false;`) when that structure likely helped reproduce compiler output.
decomp_lessons: For fighter collision routines, first look for a common skeleton: update `prev_topn`/`cur_topn`, optionally set facing direction through `mpColl_800436D8`, call a specific `mpColl_*` routine, restore `fp->cur_pos`, run `ft_80081A00`, then branch to callback, wall jump, cliff catch, landing, or fall logic.

When many adjacent functions differ only by which `mpColl_*` routine is selected and whether wall/cliff/fall follow-ups are attempted, inline helper extraction can be the difference between close code and a full match.
Header prototype cleanup is part of matching: changing `UNK_RET`, `UNK_PARAMS`, and `void*` to exact bool/void/function-pointer signatures can force correct call lowering at both callers and callees.

Callback-heavy map collision functions may need both a typed public prototype and internal casts to global callback storage, as seen in `mpColl_800476B4` storing callback/gobj into `mpColl_804D64A0` and `mpColl_804D64A4`.

Small formatting details can matter for this compiler output, including `10.0F`/`0.0F`, local aliasing of `CollData* coll`, splitting expressions into temporaries such as `box`, and retaining ternary or explicit boolean returns.
A broad PR can improve many matches while breaking a tiny existing match; bot reports should be checked for regressions like `ft_800850B4` falling to 98.64%.
naming_conventions: `Fighter_GObj* gobj` is preferred over `void*` for fighter object parameters once known.
`HSD_GObjEvent cb`/`cb2` is used for gobj callback parameters.
`ftCollisionBox* ecb` or `box` is used for ECB/collision box arguments once identified.
Unknown address-based names were retained (`ft_80082F28`, `mpColl_800476B4`) while improving type information rather than inventing semantic names.
Map collision bit flags follow `MPCOLL_FLAGS_BNN` naming, with comments documenting the hex mask.
`ftGetFacingDirInt2(Fighter_GObj* gobj)` wraps `ftGetFacingDirInt(GET_FIGHTER(gobj))` for call sites that naturally start from a gobj.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted `PAD_STACK(8)` and `PAD_STACK(16)` in multiple functions to match stack frame size/layout.
Used `RETURN_IF(...)` for early-out wall-jump/cliff/fall checks in functions where that macro likely matches branch structure.
Kept multiple nearly identical `static inline bool` helpers instead of one generic shared function, likely to satisfy inlining/codegen around similar collision paths.
Used local `CollData*` aliases and reordered initialization so `coll->prev_topn`/`coll->cur_topn` accesses generate matching code.
Split nested calls like `mpColl_8004730C(coll, ft_80082838(...))` into a local `box` temporary in `ft_800824A0`.
Changed float literals to uppercase-suffixed forms (`10.0F`, `0.0F`, `6.0F`) in matched code.
Replaced raw `0x18000`, `0x1000000`, and `0x2000000` checks with named constants without changing the underlying masks.

Made `mpColl_800473CC`, `mpColl_800474E0`, `mpColl_80047AC8`, `mpColl_80047BF4`, `mpColl_80047D20`, `mpColl_80047E14`, `mpColl_80047F40`, `mpColl_8004806C`, `mpColl_80048654`, `mpColl_8004B4B0`, and `mpColl_8004B5C4` return `bool` where caller code tests their result.

review_feedback.feedback: Lots of similar structures in these matches, so there is still a possibility of severe inlining.
GALE01 report showed +0.20% matched code, +7696 bytes, 33 new matches, 3 improved unmatched functions, and 1 broken match (`ft_800850B4`, 100.00% to 98.64%).
No human review comments were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Check later PRs that restore `ft_800850B4` from 98.64% to 100% after PR #1902.
Search for subsequent semantic renames of address-based `ft_081B` collision functions matched here.
Search for reuse of `ft_80083090_inline`, `ft_80083318_inline`, or similar static-inline collision skeletons in adjacent fighter collision files.
Audit later changes to `MPCOLL_UNK` and `MPCOLL_FLAGS_B28/B29` to see whether these flag names gained semantic names.
Look for follow-up PRs expanding `mpColl_800475F4` or surrounding `mpcoll.c` functions now that `mpColl_800476B4` is matched.
